title: Robinhood vs Light Horse: Two Different Models for AI Agent Trading
author: Stunning_Forever7090
selftext:
I’ve been using Light Horse for a while, and about two months ago they launched agent trading. I tried inside Claude. As far as I know, they were one of the first brokers to make agent trading actually usable.

Two weeks ago, Robinhood also opened up to AI agents, which feels like a pretty big signal for the whole brokerage space. Since I already have a Robinhood account, I also tried it too.

But when I compare Robinhood with Light Horse, they seem to be taking two different routes.

|**Category**|**Robinhood**|**Light Horse**|
|:-|:-|:-|
|Core idea|Add AI agents to an existing brokerage app|Build brokerage around AI assistants|
|Starting point|Robinhood app|Claude, Gemini, ChatGPT, plus the Light Horse app|
|Account model|Separate Robinhood Agentic account|Main brokerage account|
|Permission design|Agent can operate inside the dedicated Agentic account, depending on the user’s setup and permissions|User must enter a verification code before key actions go through|
|Risk boundary|Risk is isolated to the funds placed in the Agentic account|Risk is controlled through user approval before actions|
|Beyond trading|Focused on agentic trading access|Supports broader brokerage workflows like account opening, funding, tax document downloads, portfolio reports, and customer service questions|
|Biggest difference|AI gets a dedicated sandbox account to act in|AI works with the real brokerage workflow, but actions require user confirmation|

It feels more like two different visions.

Robinhood may have the advantage with mainstream users because people already know and trust the app.

Light Horse may have the more AI native workflow because the research does not need to happen inside the brokerage app. The app can stay simple, while the AI assistant handles the heavier research and decision support.

That is probably the most interesting part of the comparison.

My read: Robinhood validates the category. Light Horse is betting that the actual interface shift happens outside the traditional trading app.

Curious if anyone here has actually made money using an AI trading agent yet, or is this still mostly an interface experiment for now?
